titleStmt.title: Letter from Frances Reddy to the Lord Lieutenant of Ireland, 5 February 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Frances Reddy

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Miss Frances Reddy, Carlow, to Lord Wimbourne, Lord Lieutenant General and General Governor of Ireland, containing her application for the grand of administration and license of the public house owned by her late sister Margaret Reddy to be transferred from Margaret to herself. Ivor Churchill Guest, 1st Viscount Wimborne (1873-1939) was appointed the Lord Lieutenant of Ireland in 1915. Guest worked closely alongside Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939) in the administration of Ireland. Following the suppression of the Rising, Guest, under pressure, was forced to resign as Lord Lieutenant. A later inquiry into the Rising exonerated Guest and held Augustine Birrell (1850-1933) chiefly responsible for the events that occurred. Guest would continue to serve as Lord Lieutenant until 1918.

correspAction.persName: Lord Wimbourne, Lord Lieutenant of Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
To HIS EXCELLENCY, the Lord Lieutenant General and the Governor of Ireland. The Humble Petition of FRANCES REDDY of No. 67 Tullow Street, Carlow, in the County of Carlow, spinster, SHEWETH: Your Petitioner extracted a Grant of Administration
on the 18th January 1916 to her sister Margaret Reddy,
late of No. 67 Tullow Street aforesaid, Spinster, who died
on the 8th day of November last and said Grant is herewith
sent. The said Margaret Reddy was the proprietor of the
house know as No. 67 Tullow Street, Carlow, in respect
of which she was duly licensed for the sale of spirits,
tobacco, etc. in respect of said premises, and said licence
is also herewith sent. Your petitioner resided with her said sister, and
still resides at No. 67 Tullow Street, Carlow, aforesaid,
and the usual temporary transfer at Petty Sessions was
granted to her on the 20th December 1916. Your Petitioner made application at the Hilary
Quarter Sessions at Carlow on the 2nd day of February
1916, for a transfer of the said licence to her, and served
the usual Notices on the two nearest magistrates and pub-
lished the necessary notice in the local Newspaper, but
on the hearing of the application it was ascertained that
by an error on the part of her solicitor, the Notice of
Application had not been lodged with the Clerk of the
Crown and Peace as required by Section 2 of the 3rd and
4th Wm. IV. Chap. 68. THERE
There was no objection to the granting of said
licence on the part of the police, or by any one at
said Quarter Sessions, but the judge held that the
application was not before him until same was lodged
with the Clerk of the Crown and Peace in the regular
way, and that he, therefore, could not grant the
application. Your Petitioner, therefore, prays that she may be
allowed to keep open her business premises for the sale
of spirits etc. until the next Quarter Sessions for the
Division of Carlow which will be held on the 12th day
of April next, and at which she intends to apply for a
transfer to her of the said licence. And your Petitioner will ever pray
Signed Frances Reddy 67 Tullow Street CARLOW. 5th February 1916
